steps.text: Evaluate the acceptability of the multi-metals continuous emission monitoring system (CEMS) at the time of or soon after installation.
Calibrate, maintain, and operate the CEMS.
Conduct CEMS performance evaluations at other times besides the initial test as required by the Administrator.

preconditions.text: The CEMS must measure the particular metal and meet the performance specifications for that metal to be used to determine emission compliance.
The Administrator requires a performance evaluation under Section 114 of the Act.

constraints.text: The multi-metals CEMS must be capable of measuring the total concentrations of two or more of the specified metals (Sb, As, Ba, Be, Cd, Cr, Pb, Hg, Ag, Tl, Mn, Co, Ni, Se) in both their vapor and solid forms.
The diluent (O2) monitor must meet its own performance specifications under 40 CFR part 60, Appendix B, Performance Specification 3.
The span value shall be documented by the CEMS manufacturer with laboratory data.

steps.text: Install the CEMS at an accessible location downstream of all pollution control equipment where the metals concentrations measurements are directly representative or can be corrected so as to be representative of the total emissions from the affected facility.
Select representative measurement points or paths.
Calculate the equivalent duct diameter as per 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Section 2.1.
Select traverse measurement point locations according to 40 CFR part 60, Appendix A, Method 1, Sections 2.2 and 2.3.
Displace the RM traverse line up to 30 cm (or 5 percent of the equivalent diameter of the cross section, whichever is less) from the centroidal area.
Perform calibration at a point equal to 80 to 120 percent of the applicable emission standard for each metal.

preconditions.text: The RM traverse line interferes with or is interfered by the CEMS measurements.

constraints.text: The CEMS data recorder response range must include zero and a high level value equal to the span value (or have multiple outputs/auto-changing capability if a lower high level value is used).

The RA of the CEMS must be no greater than 20 percent of the mean value of the RM test data in terms of units of the emission standard for each metal, or 10 percent of the applicable standard, whichever is greater.
The CEMS calibration may not drift or deviate from the reference value (RV) of the calibration standard used for each metal by more than 5 percent of the emission standard for each metal.

steps.text: Install the CEMS and prepare the RM test site according to the specifications in Section 3.
Prepare the CEMS for operation according to the manufacturer’s written instructions.
Determine the calibration and zero drift.

preconditions.text: The affected facility must be operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in an applicable subpart.

constraints.text: The CEMS zero point for each metal shall not drift by more than 5 percent of the emission standard for that metal.
The CEMS shall sample the stack effluent continuously.
The response time for the CEMS must not exceed 2 minutes to achieve 95 percent of the final stable value.

steps.text: Determine the magnitude of the calibration drift (CD) and zero drift (ZD) once each day (at 24-hour intervals) for 7 consecutive days.
Conduct a Relative Accuracy (RA) test following the CD test period.
Conduct the CD test immediately before periodic automatic or manual adjustments, or conduct it in such a way that the CD and ZD can be determined.
Conduct the CD and ZD tests at the points specified in Sections 4.3 and 4.4.
Record the CEMS response.
Calculate the CD and ZD using the specified formulas.

preconditions.text: No unscheduled maintenance, repair, or manual adjustment of the CEMS took place during the preceding 24-hour period.
The affected facility is operating at more than 50 percent of normal load, or as specified in the applicable subpart.
Periodic automatic or manual adjustments are to be made to the CEMS zero and calibration settings.

constraints.text: None of the CD's or ZD's may exceed the specification.

steps.text: Conduct the RM measurements in such a way that they will yield results representative of the emissions from the source and can be correlated to the CEMS data.
Note the beginning and end of each RM test period of each run (including the exact time of day) in the CEMS data log.
Determine from the CEMS final output the integrated average pollutant concentration for each RM test period.
Confirm that the pair of results are on a consistent moisture, temperature, and diluent concentration basis.
Compare each integrated CEMS value against the corresponding average RM value.
Obtain a minimum of three pairs of CEMS and RM measurements.

preconditions.text: The concentration of each metal must be detectable by both the CEMS and the RM.
The RA must be determined at three levels (0 to 20, 40 to 60, and 80 to 120 percent of the emission limit) for one of the metals which will be monitored, or for iron.

constraints.text: Diluent and moisture measurements must be taken within a 30- to 60-minute period, which includes the pollutant measurements, to be used to calculate dry pollutant concentration.
Consider system response time, if important.

steps.text: Calculate the arithmetic mean of a data set.
Calculate the arithmetic mean of the difference, d, of a data set, using Equation 3 and substituting d for x.
Calculate the standard deviation (SD) of a data set using Equation 5.
Calculate the Relative Accuracy (RA) using Equation 6.
Summarize in tabular form the results of the CD tests and the RA tests or alternate RA procedure as appropriate.
Include all data sheets, calculations, and records of CEMS response necessary to substantiate that the performance of the CEMS met the performance specifications.
Report the CEMS measurements to the agency.

preconditions.text: Check with the appropriate regional office, or State, or local agency for additional requirements.

constraints.text: Use n equal to the number of individual values for determining the t-value from Table 1.
The CEMS measurements shall be reported in units of µg/m³ on a dry basis, corrected to 20°C or 7 percent O₂.
